qa_pairs.question: Why is it important to retain a copy of the complete directory structure for your /nearline space on the Béluga cluster, and what command can be used to do so?
qa_pairs.answer: It is important to retain a copy of the complete directory structure for your /nearline space because you will need the full path for file(s) if you need to restore them. You can do this by running the command `ls -R > ~/nearline_contents.txt` from the `~/nearline/PROJECT` directory.

qa_pairs.question: What is one key difference in how the /nearline service operates on the Nibi cluster compared to the Béluga cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: On the Nibi cluster, it may take longer than 24 hours for the first tape copy of the data to be created, which is a distinction from the Béluga cluster's service.
